The Cartographer's Silence
Part 2: The Blank

Chapter 2: Into the Blank

The edge of the mapped world was not a line on the ground. Tessa had expected — what? A wall of fog, a change in the light, some visible boundary between the known and the unknown.

Instead, the road simply became less certain. The packed dirt softened. The trees stopped being species she recognized and became species she almost recognized. Close enough to name, wrong enough to doubt.

She checked her compass. The needle was steady, which was more than she could say for her hands.
